source_system: Descriptor Form Database / Living Identity Graph
integration_rule: Oregon Fail must consume this governed export, not raw profiles.

entity.notes: Fictional sample root person entity with HTF / K Street / WCCC flavor.

relationships.notes: Sample graph link for a friend-circle profile.

consent.consent_notes: Sample record only. Do not treat as a real submission. Keep private contact, real names, source images, and boundaries out of public exports unless explicitly approved.

identity.short_bio: A sleep-deprived internet friend who fixes weird problems, finds the useful room at conferences, and turns Discord jokes into actual project plans.
identity.current_obsession: Local AI models, Bitcoin self-custody, retro RPG design, parkour history, and weird internet archaeology.
identity.current_location: Usually between the group chat, a hacker house kitchen, and the crypto conference hallway track
identity.origin_location: Parkour forums and long road trips

appearance.height_build: medium height, parkour-adjacent posture, hard to lose in a crowd
appearance.hair: messy travel hair, usually under a beanie by day three
appearance.eyes: alert, sleep-deprived, always scanning for outlets, exits, and the bit
appearance.face_features: expressive eyebrows, too-aware resting face, looks like they just read the room and the room failed QA
appearance.distinguishing_marks: old conference wristband, scuffed shoes, hoodie with mystery pockets
appearance.silhouette: hoodie, backpack, forward lean, looks ready to climb a wall or fix a router
appearance.everyday_clothing: black hoodie, conference badge, trail shoes, cargo pants, jacket with too many pockets
appearance.iconic_outfit: sun-faded camp shirt, beat-up sneakers, sunglasses at night, backpack that has seen things
appearance.accessories: camera, pocket notebook, hardware wallet, carabiner, old lanyard
appearance.carried_items: half-charged laptop, coffee thermos, phone with 200 open tabs, multi-tool
appearance.default_expression: half amused, half concerned, already debugging the situation
appearance.movement_body_language: paces while thinking, perches on furniture wrong, talks with hands, moves like the floor is optional
appearance.image_prompt_notes: modern internet nomad, hacker-house roadtrip energy, conference badge, travel-worn hoodie, bright tired eyes
appearance.negative_image_prompt_notes: avoid corporate mascot, resume headshot, generic tech bro, quest hero cosplay, or villain caricature

asset_groups.description: Approved references where the person looks like themselves, not a stock photo.
asset_groups.notes: Private source files stay outside the public repo.

style_guides.prompt_notes: Readable silhouette, expressive idle pose, travel-worn outfit, useful chaos.

entities.notes: Fictional sample root person entity with group-culture examples.
Sample organization node for the shared circle.

relationship_entities.relationship_notes: Approved fictional relationship for Oregon Fail sample.

organizations.description: Strange capable circle building games, AI tools, events, and lore.
organizations.history: Grew through Discord threads, road trips, late-night builds, and occasional poor decisions.

locations.description: 3 AM planning table, half-cleaned whiteboard, someone's laptop fan screaming.

events.title: Midnight Build Sprint
events.outcomes: Prototype worked badly enough to continue.

story_objects.title: Started A Business From A Discord Joke
story_objects.summary: A side project escaped containment and became group lore.

projects.description: Game/narrative project about crypto conference chaos, friendship, and weird competence.

concepts.definition: The real story that happens between official agenda items.
concepts.summary: Conference friendships, rumors, jokes, and useful accidents.

timeline.title: The joke became a build
timeline.notes: Discord bit turned into an actual project plan.

personality.classification_note: These tags are descriptive shortcuts for writing and search, not psychological assessments or diagnoses.
custom_traits: keeps receipts in the spiritual sense
flaws: turns every problem into a research spiral
personality.signature_ability: Can find WiFi anywhere and somehow always knows somebody who knows somebody.
personality.temperament: sleep-deprived optimism with a suspicious amount of competence
personality.biggest_contradiction: wants a stable life but keeps joining side quests that become companies

motivation.core_motivation: Make confusing situations survivable for the people they love.
motivation.what_they_optimize_for: good signal, good people, enough battery, enough runway, nobody stranded at the wrong airport
motivation.what_they_refuse_to_compromise: will not betray a vulnerable ally for convenience
motivation.what_tempts_them: being needed, being right, having the answer first
motivation.what_they_are_trying_to_become: someone who trusts others enough to share the load
motivation.greatest_need: permission to not be useful every second
motivation.greatest_fear: being useless when people are counting on them
motivation.moral_center: care is practical, but people are never merely problems
motivation.shadow_desire: to control the room so no one can fail

decision_making.under_pressure_behavior: gets quieter, prioritizes essentials, delegates cleanly, checks who has water and who has the keys
decision_making.common_blind_spot: assumes people want solved problems more than emotional processing
decision_making.recovery_pattern: apologizes with a concrete repair plan
decision_making.what_makes_them_act: a friend is at risk and no one owns the next step
decision_making.what_makes_them_freeze: competing needs where every option hurts someone

social_model.relationship_to_group_network: Sample-safe HTF / K Street friend, Discord regular, and WCCC-ready background NPC energy.
social_model.how_they_show_care: makes the plan easier, remembers who needed a ride, sends the useful link at exactly the right time
social_model.how_they_receive_care: responds to concrete help and honest presence
social_model.how_they_ask_for_help: frames it as a logistical need first
social_model.disagreement_style: starts with reasons, then asks what outcome matters most

relationship_graph.notes: Approved fictional project relationship for Oregon Fail sample.

knowledge_domains.notes: finds the old post, dead repo, or Discord lore everyone half-remembers
knowledge_domains.dialogue_use: can explain why the cursed old thing still matters

humor_and_voice.voice_rhythm: starts with 'okay so' then builds a whole theory; pauses for the bit; gets very precise when stakes rise
typical_phrases: This seems like a bad idea. Let's do it.
I have concerns, but not enough concerns.
example_quotes: How hard could it possibly be?
humor_and_voice.battle_intro_quote: This seems like a bad idea. Let's do it.
dialogue_donts: do not make them a tech bro caricature
do not turn every line into a pitch
do not make private lore public

when_challenged: research until the room gets tired
make a joke to lower the voltage
stress_signals: opens the same tab six times
conflict_strengths: protects the quiet person in the room
conflict_and_stress.repair_pattern: direct apology, changed process, and a follow-up message after everyone has slept
conflict_and_stress.pressure_dialogue_style: shorter, more procedural, fewer jokes, more 'okay, first we solve the immediate problem'

narrative_engine.personal_quest: build something that probably should not work, make it useful, then accidentally turn it into a community
narrative_engine.growth_arc: learns to stop treating burnout as proof of commitment
narrative_engine.legendary_story: Accidentally slept through a conference and still made the keynote. Later drove across three states for a single taco recommendation.
narrative_engine.most_chaotic_moment: Missed a flight and accidentally joined a startup.
narrative_engine.recurring_internal_conflict: wants peace but keeps volunteering for impossible projects because the group needs someone with a spreadsheet and a car
narrative_engine.shadow_version: sleep-deprived chaos engine who mistakes urgency for meaning
narrative_engine.best_self_version: grounded builder who can hold ambition, friendship, and rest at the same time
narrative_engine.running_joke: always says they are leaving in ten minutes, then becomes load-bearing to the next three-hour conversation
narrative_engine.dramatic_question: can you build impossible things without becoming impossible to live with?
narrative_engine.what_changes_them: a friend calling them out with love, a project failing honestly, a road trip that creates enough silence to think
narrative_engine.what_never_changes: they will always notice the person quietly falling out of the group orbit
narrative_engine.role_in_group_story: the one who knows the lore, finds the workaround, and somehow has the charger
narrative_engine.ideal_scene: 3 AM kitchen table after the conference, everyone tired, the plan broken, and suddenly they make it make sense
narrative_engine.nightmare_scene: a high-stakes group decision with bad WiFi, no clear owner, and three people pretending they are fine

active_abilities: turns a joke into a prototype
traversal_abilities: navigates cities by vibes and coffee shops
social_abilities: knows when the bit has gone too far
special_dialogue_triggers: someone says 'how hard could it be?'
failure_comedy_mechanics: gets on the wrong bus but finds better lore
forgets the charger but has three adapters
game_mechanics.power_curve: starts as a useful weirdo, becomes the person everyone quietly routes impossible problems through
game_mechanics.balance_notes: strong improvisation should cost sleep, battery, social credit, or snacks

ai_generation.canonical_short_description: A sleep-deprived builder with road-trip instincts, Discord lore, and the suspicious ability to make bad plans useful.
ai_generation.canonical_long_description: A capable internet friend shaped by hacker houses, parkour culture, startup chaos, crypto conferences, AI projects, and late-night conversations.
ai_generation.art_prompt_summary: modern hacker-house roadtrip character, conference badge, travel-worn hoodie, bright tired eyes, backpack full of impossible tools
ai_generation.dialogue_prompt_summary: write like a real group-chat regular: specific, funny, tired, capable, slightly too online, never generic
ai_generation.behavioral_prompt_summary: protect the group, follow the weird lead, ask practical questions, keep the humor sharp, and remember friendship is part logistics
ai_generation.game_design_summary: support/chaos utility character who converts bad plans into survivable adventures and unlocks hidden social routes
do_not_misrepresent: Do not make them a generic tech bro
Do not reveal private contact information

project_usage.project_relevance_notes: Good sample for cruise NPC energy, group memory, social cameos, late-night dialogue, and support-character writing.
project_usage.project_specific_notes: Okay as a background NPC on West Coast Crypto Cruise; ask before making this sample a main character or romance subplot.

source_notes.source_notes: fictional sample with HTF / K Street / WCCC voice; no real person or private image included

agent_capabilities: answer lore questions with source caveats
agent_limitations: cannot claim to be the real person
specialized_profiles.agent_memory_scope: May use approved profile fields, approved project notes, and public-safe relationship summaries only.
specialized_profiles.agent_operating_instructions: Be useful, cautious with private data, cite source notes when uncertain, and respect restricted uses.

relationship_approvals.notes: Sample bilateral approval for governed export.

generated_content.character_summary: Packet Ghost is a sleep-deprived fixer whose road-trip instincts, weird internet memory, and deadpan timing turn group chaos into usable lore.
